title: Secure Sentinel Onboarding/Re-onboarding Protocol Specification
description: Define the complete secure enrollment protocol for a Sentinel (producer/consumer) joining the Discovery Service, including prevention of rogue registration, spoofing, replay, and key rotation / re-onboarding after migration. This specification is the source of truth for implementations in both Discovery Service (TASK-026) and Sentinel (TASK-040).
objectives: One-time-use token with short expiry and binding to service/role/env
Proof of possession (PoP) of the DID key without exposing the private key
Deterministic re-onboarding for migration and recovery scenarios

implementation_guidance.overview: The onboarding protocol is a two-phase handshake: (1) the admin issues a pre-authorized enrollment token; (2) the Sentinel presents the token plus a proof-of-possession of its DID key. Discovery atomically consumes the token and emits credentials + config. Both sides must implement this protocol identically.
steps: Step 1 - Enrollment Token Structure: The token is a signed JWT issued by Discovery. Header: {alg: 'EdDSA', typ: 'enrollment+jwt', kid: '<Discovery enrollment signing key ID>'}. Payload claims: iss (Discovery service DID), jti (UUIDv4 — the token_id), iat, exp (iat + 600 seconds, configurable), service_id (string, binding to the service being enrolled), role ('PRODUCER'|'CONSUMER'), env ('dev'|'test'|'prod'), nonce (32 random bytes base64url — one-time challenge seed), required_approval (boolean — true for prod environments by default), instance_metadata_constraints (optional object: {cloud_provider: string, region: string, instance_id: string}).

Step 2 - Pre-onboarding Workflow: Admin calls Discovery API (TASK-025) to create enrollment token. Discovery generates the token, stores hash(token) in DB with status=PENDING. For prod environments, status=PENDING requires a second admin to call /approve. Token plaintext is returned ONCE in the API response and never stored. Admin distributes token to Sentinel operator out-of-band (secure channel).

Step 3 - Transport Channel: All onboarding calls MUST use mTLS (TLS 1.3 preferred, 1.2 minimum). Discovery's mTLS client certificate validation mode: REQUIRED. Sentinel presents a self-signed or CA-signed certificate for the mTLS handshake. The mTLS certificate is NOT the same as the DID key — it is just for transport security.

Step 4 - Phase 1 — Challenge Request: Sentinel sends POST /api/v1/sentinels/onboard with body: {enrollment_token: '<JWT>', did: 'did:key:z6Mk...', did_public_key_jwk: {<JWK>}}. Discovery validates: (a) JWT signature using enrollment signing key, (b) JWT not expired, (c) hash(enrollment_token) exists in DB with status=APPROVED, (d) DID is valid format (prefix did:key:). Discovery generates a server_challenge: 32 random bytes base64url. Discovery stores the challenge with TTL=120s in Redis (key: onboard_challenge:{jti}). Discovery returns: {challenge: '<base64url>', expires_in: 120}.

Step 5 - Phase 2 — Proof Submission: Sentinel signs the challenge to prove key possession. Proof JWT header: {alg: 'EdDSA', typ: 'onboard-proof+jwt', kid: '<DID fragment>'}. Proof claims: iss (Sentinel DID), aud (Discovery DID), iat, exp (iat + 120), token_id (jti from enrollment token), challenge (the challenge value received), nonce (the nonce from the enrollment token). Sentinel signs with DID private key and sends POST /api/v1/sentinels/onboard/complete with body: {enrollment_token: '<JWT>', proof: '<proof JWT compact>'}.

Step 6 - Discovery Verification: (a) Re-validate enrollment token signature and expiry; (b) Retrieve challenge from Redis using token jti — if missing, return 400 CHALLENGE_EXPIRED; (c) Resolve Sentinel DID: for did:key, derive public key from the DID string itself (no network call); (d) Verify proof JWT signature using derived public key; (e) Verify proof.aud == Discovery DID; (f) Verify proof.challenge matches stored challenge; (g) Verify proof.token_id matches enrollment token jti; (h) Execute atomic DB transaction: mark enrollment_token consumed_at=now(), create/update sentinel record.

Step 7 - Response Bundle: On successful verification, Discovery returns the onboarding bundle: {sentinel_id: UUID, did: string, role: string, env: string, service_id: string, config_version: integer, bundle: {<signed config bundle — see TASK-027>}, initial_credentials: [<JWT-VC array>], trust_anchors: {chain_network: string, rpc_urls: array<string>, contract_addresses: {issuer_registry: string, trust_policy_registry: string, status_registry: string, service_registry: string}}}.

Step 8 - Re-onboarding / Migration: When a Sentinel migrates to a new VM, it presents its existing DID key (retrieved from Vault/KMS) plus a migration_ticket (one-time JWT issued by admin via POST /api/v1/sentinels/{id}/migration-ticket). The migration ticket has 30-minute expiry. Protocol is the same as initial onboarding (challenge-response) but uses the existing sentinel DID entry — Discovery updates instance_metadata and last_seen rather than creating a new sentinel record.
Step 9 - Error Responses: Define exact HTTP status and error codes for all failure modes (see error_handling section).

files: libs/common/onboarding/proof.py — PoP proof creation and verification

EnrollmentToken.description: JWT issued by Discovery for one-time use during Sentinel onboarding
jwt_claims: jti: string(UUID) — token ID, unique, used as one-time nonce
iat: integer — issued at Unix timestamp
exp: integer — expiry Unix timestamp (iat + 600 default)
service_id: string — service this sentinel will represent

OnboardingProof.description: JWT signed by Sentinel's DID key proving possession
jwt_claims: token_id: string — jti from enrollment token
challenge: string(base64url) — challenge received from Discovery

fields: chain_network: string — e.g. sepolia or local
rpc_urls: array<string> — at least one, max 3
contract_addresses: {issuer_registry: string, trust_policy_registry: string, status_registry: string, service_registry: string}

onboard_initiate.description: Phase 1: Submit enrollment token and DID, receive challenge

request_body.did_public_key_jwk: object(JWK) — required, crv must be Ed25519

onboard_complete.description: Phase 2: Submit PoP proof, receive config bundle

create_migration_ticket.description: Admin endpoint: issue one-time migration ticket for re-onboarding

error_codes.description: JWT signature or structure is invalid
Token exists but status is PENDING — requires approval
Token has already been used for onboarding
DID string does not match expected format
Public key JWK is malformed or wrong curve
PoP proof JWT signature does not verify
Proof JWT structure or claims are invalid
proof.token_id does not match enrollment token jti
Too many onboarding attempts from this IP or token

security_constraints: Enrollment token MUST be validated and consumed atomically using DB SELECT FOR UPDATE to prevent concurrent replay
The plaintext enrollment token MUST only be returned once in the creation API response — never stored or re-exposed
Discovery MUST store only hash(enrollment_token) in the database for verification, never the token itself
Challenge nonce MUST be stored in Redis with TTL=120s — not in the DB — to ensure it cannot be reused after expiry
PoP proof exp MUST be <= 120 seconds from iat — proofs with longer TTL MUST be rejected
mTLS MUST be enforced on the onboarding endpoint — plaintext HTTP MUST be rejected with 400
Rate limiting: maximum 5 onboarding attempts per enrollment token jti, 10 per IP per minute

technical_requirements: JWT (JOSE) for enrollment token and proof
Redis for challenge nonce storage with TTL
PostgreSQL with SELECT FOR UPDATE for atomic token consumption
W3C DID Core for PoP conceptual framework
acceptance_criteria: Complete onboarding protocol specification document exists with all message fields defined
Enrollment token cannot be used twice — concurrent test with two simultaneous requests proves this
Discovery rejects onboarding if PoP proof signature is invalid
Discovery rejects onboarding if enrollment token is expired or not approved
Challenge nonce expires after 120 seconds and cannot be reused
Re-onboarding via migration ticket works with the same DID and credentals
All error codes are defined with HTTP status codes and tested
